elements.description: Main navigation link to the Home page
elements.aria_notes: Link text is exactly 'Home'. Multiple pages have this nav; on the home page it also has aria-current='page'.

elements.description: Main navigation link to the Services page
elements.aria_notes: Link text is exactly 'Services'.

elements.description: Main navigation link to the Contact page
elements.aria_notes: Link text is exactly 'Contact'.

elements.description: Primary CTA button on the homepage hero section linking to contact.html
elements.aria_notes: Visually styled as a button but semantically an <a> link. getByRole('link') is correct; getByRole('button') will not find it.

elements.description: Learn more link on the Strategy service card
elements.aria_notes: All three 'Learn more →' links have identical visible text. Must disambiguate by position or scope within the parent article.

elements.description: Learn more link on the Technology service card
elements.aria_notes: Filtering by article containing 'Technology' text is the robust approach when link text is duplicated.

elements.description: Learn more link on the Operations service card
elements.aria_notes: Same duplicate text trap as strategy and technology links.

elements.description: Full name input on the contact form
elements.aria_notes: Has explicit <label for='full-name'>. getByLabel matches the label text exactly.

elements.description: Work email input on the contact form
elements.aria_notes: Has explicit <label for='work-email'>. type='email' means it may not match getByRole('textbox') in all Playwright versions — getByLabel is safer.

elements.description: Company input on the contact form
elements.aria_notes: Has explicit <label for='company'>.

elements.description: Phone input on the contact form — optional, no required attribute
elements.aria_notes: Has label text 'Phone (optional)'. getByLabel('Phone') matches because it does a substring check by default.
intentional_traps: optional_field_trap: no required attribute — asserting toHaveValue on an unfilled phone field returns '' not an error

elements.description: Service interest select/combobox on the contact form
elements.aria_notes: First option has value='' and text 'Select a service…'. Players must call selectOption() explicitly; the default value is empty string.
intentional_traps: empty_default_option_trap: default value is '' not a service name — asserting toHaveValue without selecting will fail

elements.description: Message textarea on the contact form
elements.aria_notes: Has explicit <label for='message'>.

elements.description: Submit button on the contact form
elements.aria_notes: type='submit'. Accessible name is 'Send message'.

elements.description: Confirmation alert shown after successful form submission
elements.aria_notes: role='alert' with aria-live='polite'. Only becomes visible after form submission (display:none → visible class). toBeVisible() assertion is correct.

elements.description: Request a consultation button in the Strategy section on the services page
elements.aria_notes: aria-haspopup='dialog'. Three buttons share the same label — must scope to the section by id.
intentional_traps: duplicate_button_name_trap: three buttons with identical text, must scope to #strategy section

elements.description: Request a consultation button in the Technology section on the services page
elements.aria_notes: aria-haspopup='dialog'. Must scope to #technology to be unambiguous.
intentional_traps: duplicate_button_name_trap: three buttons with identical text, must scope to #technology section

elements.description: Request a consultation button in the Operations section on the services page
elements.aria_notes: aria-haspopup='dialog'. Must scope to #operations to be unambiguous.
intentional_traps: duplicate_button_name_trap: three buttons with identical text, must scope to #operations section

elements.description: Modal dialog for requesting a Strategy consultation
elements.aria_notes: role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby='modal-strategy-title'. The accessible name is derived from the heading via aria-labelledby.
intentional_traps: aria_labelledby_trap: dialog accessible name comes from the heading id, not inner text — getByRole('dialog', { name: 'Request a Strategy Consultation' }) is the canonical locator

elements.description: Modal dialog for requesting a Technology consultation
elements.aria_notes: role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby='modal-technology-title'.
intentional_traps: aria_labelledby_trap: must use { name: 'Request a Technology Consultation' } to distinguish from other dialogs

elements.description: Modal dialog for requesting an Operations consultation
elements.aria_notes: role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby='modal-operations-title'.
intentional_traps: aria_labelledby_trap: note 'an' not 'a' in the heading — 'Request an Operations Consultation'

elements.description: Close button inside any Thornfield modal dialog
elements.aria_notes: aria-label='Close dialog'. Visible text is '×' (multiplication sign) which is unreliable as a locator; use aria-label.
intentional_traps: icon_button_trap: visible text is '×' not 'Close' — must use aria-label

elements.description: Name input inside a modal dialog
elements.aria_notes: Label text is 'Your name' (different from contact form 'Full name'). Must scope to the dialog to avoid ambiguity when multiple dialogs are in DOM.
intentional_traps: scoping_trap: all three modals are in the DOM simultaneously — must scope locator to the open dialog

elements.description: Email input inside a modal dialog
elements.aria_notes: Label text is 'Your email'. All three modal email inputs are in the DOM simultaneously — must scope to the open dialog.
intentional_traps: scoping_trap: must scope to open dialog, otherwise locator matches three hidden inputs

elements.description: Submit request button inside a modal dialog
elements.aria_notes: type='submit', text 'Submit request'. Must scope to dialog to avoid matching other modal submit buttons.

elements.description: Confirmation alert shown after submitting a modal form
elements.aria_notes: role='alert' scoped inside the dialog. All three are in DOM; must scope to the correct dialog.
intentional_traps: scoping_trap: multiple role='alert' elements in DOM — must scope to open dialog
